neighbors.name: Kovan Mountjoy
neighbors.claims: Kovan Mountjoy is listed as a contact and co-owner of ABM Development LLC, a family-run real estate development and general contracting firm based in Huachuca City, Arizona ([ABM Development LLC](https://www.abmdevelopment.com/contact-us)). The company has been operating in the area since 1994, providing custom home building, excavation, and land clearing services ([ABM Development LLC](https://www.abmdevelopment.com/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Local business owner; no civic office found

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage via an industry peer or local business forum, emphasizing how the project can create local construction jobs and maintain area property values to align with his development interests.

citations.claim: Kovan Mountjoy is listed as a contact and co-owner of ABM Development LLC
citations.title: ABM Development LLC - Contact Us

citations.claim: The company has been operating in the area since 1994
citations.title: ABM Development LLC - Home

neighbors.verification_notes: Verified business ownership and operational history via company website. Updated 'mid-1990s' to specific '1994' date found in source.

neighbors.name: Michelle White
neighbors.claims: Public records list a Michelle White in Safford, AZ, suggesting she may not reside on her Cochise County property ([Yellowbook](https://www.yellowbook.com/people/michelle-white/arizona/)). No public statements, civic roles, or attendance at local meetings were found for her in Cochise County, indicating a low public profile.

approach_recommendations.engage: Reach out by mail or phone to this absentee owner, highlighting assurances (e.g., tax benefits or stable land values) to address any concerns she may have about the development.

citations.claim: Public records list a Michelle White in Safford, AZ
citations.title: Yellowbook Listing

neighbors.verification_notes: Removed specific age reference for privacy. Could not definitively confirm Safford residency with available snippets, but retained draft citation as plausible absentee owner evidence. No local Cochise footprint found.

neighbors.name: Frank Cullen Brophy
neighbors.claims: In 1935, Frank Cullen Brophy acquired the historic Babacomari Ranch, which remains the largest contiguous private land parcel in Arizona ([Babacomari Ranch History](https://babacomariranch.com/history)). The Brophy family has placed over 3,300 acres of the ranch under conservation easements to protect grasslands and wetlands ([Arizona Land and Water Trust](https://alwt.org/trust-protects-historic-babacomari-ranch/)). In 2024, the family obtained a conditional use permit to develop a guest ranch on 2,200 acres of the property, a project estimated to cost between $40-60 million ([Patagonia Regional Times](https://patagoniaregionaltimes.org/new-40-60-million-dude-ranch-gets-first-permit/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Prominent ranching family; largest private land parcel in AZ

approach_recommendations.engage: Approach through a respected local rancher or conservation contact, emphasizing the project’s environmental stewardship and any legacy benefits (e.g., land conservation measures) to align with the Brophy family’s preservation values.

citations.claim: In 1935, Frank Cullen Brophy acquired the historic Babacomari Ranch
citations.title: Babacomari Ranch History

citations.claim: The Brophy family has placed over 3,300 acres of the ranch under conservation easements
citations.title: Trust Protects Historic Babacomari Ranch

citations.claim: In 2024, the family obtained a conditional use permit to develop a guest ranch on 2,200 acres
citations.title: New $40-60 Million Dude Ranch Gets First Permit

neighbors.verification_notes: Added details regarding the 2024 guest ranch permit to demonstrate active development interests. While 'Babacomari Solar North LLC' exists as a developer entity, no direct evidence of the family's stance on *that* specific project was found in the text, though the name implies a relationship.

neighbors.name: Ricktor and Tawneen Meldrum
neighbors.claims: Ricktor K. Meldrum is a licensed aircraft mechanic who previously lived in Logan, Utah, before acquiring land in Cochise County ([AirportGuide](https://airportguide.com/airmen/A2209738/RICKTOR-K-MELDRUM)). A search of local records found no mention of Ricktor or Tawneen Meldrum in Cochise County news or meetings, suggesting they keep a low public profile.
neighbors.influence_justification: New to area; no civic presence

approach_recommendations.engage: Make a personal, neighborly outreach (through a trusted community member if possible), and emphasize that the project will respect their rural lifestyle and peace and quiet on their property.
